summary: DHH recounts a near-disaster where Greymatter, his blogging software, wiped his entire posting history and reset his blog back to entry 1. While a rendered archive copy survived, he needs to scrape and restore the data manually. The experience convinces him to abandon Greymatter entirely and migrate to either MovableType or a mysterious new blogging tool he's been developing with a collaborator.
key_points: Greymatter blogging software catastrophically reset his 1+ year archive of posts
The incident highlights the fragility of early blogging platforms and inadequate backup systems
DHH hints at building a new blogging tool with a collaborator — likely an early reference to what would become Signal vs. Noise or related 37signals work
One catastrophic failure is enough to permanently lose trust in a software tool
money_quotes: Greymatter has just decided to do away with it all and restart my posting history at 1.
In an age that still haven't reached the point of omnipotent backups, one good scare is all it takes.
Sorry Noah, but in an age that still haven't reached the point of omnipotent backups, one good scare is all it takes.
key_insight: A single catastrophic data loss event is sufficient reason to abandon a tool entirely, especially when reliable backups don't yet exist.
